content.assessment: This proposal deliberately avoids altering the structural tracking mechanism, instead pursuing compensatory funding and quality-levelling measures. Under the Polish lens, this represents the inverse of Poland's core reform mechanism: Poland's documented gains stemmed from changing the structural selection point itself, not from compensatory funding layered onto an unchanged structure. The Polish evidence record offers no support or contradiction for a compensatory-only approach since it is structurally a different intervention type; the lens's applicability here is limited. What the Polish case does illustrate, by contrast, is that structural mechanism change was associated with measurable distributional effects, suggesting that leaving the structural mechanism untouched — as this proposal does — forgoes the specific lever Polish evidence associates with the strongest documented equity movement in the record.
opportunities: Could function as a complementary layer alongside future structural reform, similar to how Polish reform outcomes might have been sustained or improved if paired with resource-equalization measures rather than reversed structurally
strengths: By not altering the tracking structure, this proposal avoids the reversibility and implementation-shock risks that Poland's 2017 structural reversal made evident (F-live-polish_reform-04, F-live-polish_reform-05)
threats: Absent structural change, distributional gaps that Polish evidence links to selection-age timing may persist regardless of funding interventions, since funding does not alter the selection point itself (F-live-polish_reform-03)
weaknesses: Does not employ the structural mechanism (delayed tracking) that Polish evidence associates with documented socio-economic gap reductions between 2000-2015, leaving the most evidenced lever in this comparative record unused (F-live-polish_reform-03)
The Polish case does not provide evidence for or against compensatory-only approaches, so this lens cannot validate the proposal's central causal claim
